steps.title: Look for popularity evidence
steps.content: The goal is to emphasize how popular The Woman in White became when first published. The strongest note is the record-breaking magazine sales of 100,000 copies per issue.

steps.title: Reject background details
steps.content:
• Choice B explains why readers anticipated installments but does not quantify popularity.
  • Choice C gives the publication schedule.
  • Choice D gives genre and publication venue without showing popularity.

steps.title: Use the sales measure
steps.content: The remaining choice connects the novel's popularity to record-breaking magazine sales, giving a concrete measure of how popular it became. The answer is A.
